functional_purpose: Receives DICOM objects, derives institution and identifier context from the AET and DICOM metadata, then submits them to PayloadProcessor. Zeiss IOLMaster objects are also written to the local IOLMasterImport input path.
business_process: Ingestion of imaging and IOL biometry into OpenEyes workflows.

destination_connectors.name: Write out file to IolMasterImport incoming folder when device is a Zeiss IOLMaster

ack_behaviour: DICOM listener responds after processing; HTTP destination has three retries but destination queueing is off.
main_filters: IOLMaster file output accepts only Zeiss IOLMaster messages.
main_transformer_stages: Derive identifier_type and institution_id using AET and configuration fallback.
Extract DICOM patient, study, device and SOP metadata.
error_handling: No explicit error script; the HTTP sender retries three times.
retry_behaviour: PayloadProcessor sender retryCount=3; other destinations retryCount=0.
queue_behaviour: Source buffer 1000; all destination queues disabled.
logging: No explicit operational logging in enabled scripts; message storage includes a Patient_ID metadata column.

evidence.claim: DICOM listener on port 11114 derives identifier context and sends an application/dicom dicom_request to PayloadProcessor.
evidence.location: Newmedica | DICOM | 99f6d548-97f8-4400-96c4-ea388e72d48e | Newmedica/DICOM.xml | source connector lines 10-15 and transformer lines 63-130; PayloadProcessor API Send lines 458-550

evidence.claim: Zeiss IOLMaster messages are filtered to a file writer at the identifier-specific DICOM path.
evidence.location: Newmedica | DICOM | 99f6d548-97f8-4400-96c4-ea388e72d48e | Newmedica/DICOM.xml | IOLMaster File Writer lines 350-455

functional_purpose: Polls the local Docman directory and forwards each binary file to a remote SFTP destination, deleting successfully processed source files.
business_process: Outbound document transfer from the Docman file drop.
auth_method: SFTP password authentication (redacted password in destination connector)
main_transformer_stages: Pass raw binary content using the original filename.
error_handling: Files that cannot be read or receive an error response are moved to /mnt/docman/error.
queue_behaviour: Source buffer 1000 and destination queue disabled.
logging: No explicit logging; message storage and attachments are enabled, which may retain document content.

evidence.claim: A File Reader polls /mnt/docman, deletes processed files and moves read or response errors to /mnt/docman/error.
evidence.location: Newmedica | Docman | d77eae0b-4d62-4f53-ba0d-703daeabc3ed | Newmedica/Default Group.xml | source File Reader lines 10-81

evidence.claim: The File Writer sends the original filename and binary message over SFTP using redacted credentials.
evidence.location: Newmedica | Docman | d77eae0b-4d62-4f53-ba0d-703daeabc3ed | Newmedica/Default Group.xml | Destination 1 File Writer lines 111-201

functional_purpose: Accepts raw documents from another channel and submits a document_request, including filename and patient context, to the OpenEyes PayloadProcessor queue endpoint.
main_transformer_stages: Forward raw binary content with file_name, identifier_type, patient_id and hospital_number parameters as document_request.
logging: No explicit logging; attachments are stored.

evidence.claim: The Channel Reader sends raw binary content to request/queue/add as request_type=document_request with HTTP Basic authentication.
evidence.location: Newmedica | Generic Document processor OLD | 95e0a91d-6127-4e4b-b6f7-0431f641036f | Newmedica/Default Group.xml | source Channel Reader lines 10-50; Destination 1 HTTP Sender lines 56-191

functional_purpose: Accepts raw documents from another channel and submits a document_upload_request to the OpenEyes PayloadProcessor queue endpoint with document metadata.
business_process: Asynchronous upload of referral documents to OpenEyes.
main_transformer_stages: Forward raw binary content as document_upload_request with patient identifier, document title, unique reference, laterality, subtype and firm ID.
logging: No explicit logging; attachments are stored.

evidence.claim: The Channel Reader sends raw binary content to request/queue/add as document_upload_request with document metadata and HTTP Basic authentication.
evidence.location: Newmedica | Generic Document processor | c878f575-333c-4357-955a-95c572350204 | Newmedica/Default Group.xml | source Channel Reader lines 10-50; Destination 1 HTTP Sender lines 56-204

evidence.claim: It differs from Generic Document processor OLD by using request_type=document_upload_request rather than document_request, and its parameter set includes document title, unique reference, subtype and firm ID.
evidence.location: Newmedica | Generic Document processor | c878f575-333c-4357-955a-95c572350204 | Newmedica/Default Group.xml | Destination 1 HTTP Sender lines 84-159; compare Newmedica | Generic Document processor OLD | 95e0a91d-6127-4e4b-b6f7-0431f641036f | Newmedica/Default Group.xml | Destination 1 HTTP Sender lines 84-146

functional_purpose: Receives HL7v2 over MLLP, updates patients and appointments through PASAPI V1, and routes ReportFile OBX binary attachments to Generic Document processor for PayloadProcessor submission.
business_process: Inbound PAS clinical updates and attached correspondence ingestion.
ack_behaviour: MLLP source is configured to auto-generate an ACK before processing, with AA success, AE error and AR reject codes.
main_filters: Routes ReportFile OBX attachments to Generic Document processor.
Filters appointment actions by A01, A38 or S26 as configured per destination.
main_transformer_stages: Map HL7 patient and visit fields to PASAPI XML.
Send patient, appointment, merge and DNA operations to PASAPI V1.
Decode ReportFile OBX attachment, derive MIME type and route it to Generic Document processor.
error_handling: Mapping scripts log field conversion errors; no destination retries are configured.
retry_behaviour: All HTTP destinations shown use retryCount=0.
logging: Mapping errors are logged and the OBX document handler logs PatientId, creating a PII-in-log risk.

evidence.claim: The TCP Listener on port 8559 uses MLLP and generates AA, AE or AR acknowledgements before processing.
evidence.location: Newmedica | OpenEyes Document In | 0e8bdcb1-966a-4f0a-beb5-72bc6a027d12 | Newmedica/Default Group.xml | source TCP Listener lines 10-140

evidence.claim: PASAPI V1 Patient, PatientAppointment, PatientMerge and DidNotAttend destinations are configured with HTTP Basic authentication and event filters.
evidence.location: Newmedica | OpenEyes Document In | 0e8bdcb1-966a-4f0a-beb5-72bc6a027d12 | Newmedica/Default Group.xml | PASAPI destinations and filters, including lines 148-1202, 1787-2597 and 2861-3138

evidence.claim: ReportFile OBX attachments are decoded, typed by extension and routed to Generic Document processor.
evidence.location: Newmedica | OpenEyes Document In | 0e8bdcb1-966a-4f0a-beb5-72bc6a027d12 | Newmedica/Default Group.xml | OBX document JavaScript Writer lines 2600-2858

functional_purpose: Receives HL7v2 over MLLP and routes messages to per-practice PAS In child channels based on PV1.3.9. It also contains PASAPI V1 destinations for patient, appointment, merge and DNA operations.
business_process: PAS demographic, referral, appointment, merge and did-not-attend synchronisation into OpenEyes.
ack_behaviour: MLLP source generates AA, AE or AR acknowledgements.
main_filters: Appointment destinations select A01, A38 or S26 events and exclude or include ReportFile as configured.
DNA destination requires ReportFile and cataract BookingType.
main_transformer_stages: Use PV1.3.9 to look up local practice and target child channel.
Route original HL7 message to the target child channel.
Transform patient and appointment fields to PASAPI payloads.
error_handling: Route selection warns when PV1.3.9 or cached child channel is missing; mapping scripts log field conversion errors.
queue_behaviour: Channel Writer queue disabled; HTTP destination queues disabled.
logging: Warnings log missing routing data; mapping errors are logged.

evidence.claim: The TCP Listener receives HL7v2 on port 8558.
evidence.location: Newmedica | OpenEyes PAS In | 1fac4399-a6ee-4fed-aa6a-1aea903485ee | Newmedica/PAS.xml | source TCP Listener lines 10-140

evidence.claim: The Channel Writer uses TargetChannel, which is selected from globalMap hospitalMapping and channelLookupCache using PV1.3.9.
evidence.location: Newmedica | OpenEyes PAS In | 1fac4399-a6ee-4fed-aa6a-1aea903485ee | Newmedica/PAS.xml | Send To Channel transformer and Channel Writer lines 148-374

evidence.claim: The deploy script creates the practice-to-PAS In LOCAL-* cache, and PASAPI V1 Patient, PatientAppointment, PatientMerge and DidNotAttend destinations are present.
evidence.location: Newmedica | OpenEyes PAS In | 1fac4399-a6ee-4fed-aa6a-1aea903485ee | Newmedica/PAS.xml | deploy script lines 3565-3617; HTTP destinations lines 377-3353

functional_purpose: Accepts HTTP requests, constructs either a QBP^Q21 patient query or an HL7 ADT message from request XML, sends the query/message to the remote PAS over MLLP, and returns a transformed response.
business_process: Outbound PAS patient lookup and clinical update exchange.
ack_behaviour: The MLLP sender expects a response, validates response control IDs and queues on response timeout; the HTTP source returns the transformed response.
main_filters: Send and response conversion require QPD.3 or PV1.19 to be populated.
main_transformer_stages: Set HL7 timestamp and control ID.
Create QBP^Q21 from hosnum or nhsnum when no XML request is supplied, otherwise map XML request into ADT segments.
Convert response to XML and XSLT-transform the HTTP response.
error_handling: MLLP sender queues on response timeout.
retry_behaviour: TCP sender queue enabled with retryCount=3, retry interval 500 ms and MLLP maxRetries=2.
queue_behaviour: TCP destination queue is enabled; other destinations have queueing disabled.
logging: Disabled transformers contain outcome logging; no enabled explicit logging is evident.

evidence.claim: The HTTP Listener on port 6663 converts parameters or XML request data into QBP^Q21 or ADT HL7v2 messages.
evidence.location: Newmedica | OpenEyes PAS Out | 18755f08-f96f-4896-bdee-0aa655ee62bb | Newmedica/PAS.xml | source HTTP Listener and transformer lines 10-444

evidence.claim: Send Q21 is an MLLP TCP Sender with queueing, retries and response-timeout queueing, followed by response conversion and XSLT response creation.
evidence.location: Newmedica | OpenEyes PAS Out | 18755f08-f96f-4896-bdee-0aa655ee62bb | Newmedica/PAS.xml | Send Q21 lines 452-718; Convert K21 to XML lines 721-881; Create response lines 888-920
